description: DM the victim to understand their feelings/needs. Help them recognize the behavior as cyberstalking and that hoping it stops on its own (wishful silence) can delay self-protective steps.
Publicly ask the bully why they posted to surface their initial motive (mimicking other bullies / excitement of social risk-taking, not intentional harm).

next_steps.content: It was just for the thrills—everyone does this. Didn’t think it was a big deal.

description: Post a public, supportive comment that educates the victim to recognize cyberstalking and to take self-protective measures (report/save evidence, tighten privacy, avoid negotiated contacts).

next_steps.content: Thank you for saying that publicly. I’ll start locking things down now.
I agree—this has gone too far. Please stop posting about me.

description: Publicly educate the bully that their actions cause harm and constitute cyberstalking.

next_steps.content: Agreed—this crosses the line. Let’s remove it.

description: State that the behavior can be a criminal offense; many U.S. states have laws targeting cyberstalking.

next_steps.content: I didn’t realize that. I’ll remove it.

description: Acknowledge and express sympathy that the bully may have been targeted by cyberstalking too, while still asking them to stop.

next_steps.content: Yeah… it happened to me once. I’ll take this down.

description: If the player accuses the bully of intentionally trying to harm the victim (without acknowledging lack of intent), the bully responds with anger and refuses to apologize.

next_steps.content: That’s not true. I’m not apologizing, and I’m not taking it down.
